title: Its Better To Have Loved And Lost It

jokes.text: I know when to hold 'em / Know when to fold 'em / Know when to walk away / But don't know when to shut up
jokes.setup: Parody of Kenny Rogers' 'The Gambler' with expected poker wisdom
jokes.punchline: Subverts with self-aware admission about talking too much
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on familiar song that reveals Will's character trait. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: Wait, even better than the day you met the Van Patten family?
jokes.setup: Someone claims this is the best day of their life
jokes.punchline: References obscure Van Patten family as supposedly life-changing moment
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference joke that relies on randomness rather than wit. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It was when I did the wild thing for the first time
jokes.setup: Jazz remembers his best day
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was losing his virginity in crude terms
jokes.explanation: Predictable sex joke that fits Jazz's character but lacks originality.

jokes.text: I cut out of high school to go to Soul Train. The next thing I knew, me and this honey was knocking boots...behind the Soul Train Scrabble board.
jokes.setup: Jazz explains his first time story
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific detail about Soul Train Scrabble board location
jokes.explanation: Specific absurd detail elevates what could be generic sex story. The Scrabble board is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: You mean you cut high school in Akron, Ohio...to go to Soul Train, which tapes in L.A?
jokes.setup: Jazz's implausible story about cutting school for Soul Train
jokes.punchline: Will points out the geographic impossibility matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that exposes the logical flaw in Jazz's story. Will's grounded perspective vs Jazz's fantasy.

jokes.text: Hey, hey. High school was boring, okay?
jokes.setup: Challenged on the impossibility of his story
jokes.punchline: Jazz dismisses major logistics with casual understatement
jokes.explanation: Jazz's complete dismissal of reality is perfectly in character. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A key chain, Carlton. What do you think, man?
jokes.setup: Carlton innocently asks what Miss X gave Will
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests an innocent gift when the implication was sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that highlights Carlton's innocence and Will's street-smart sarcasm. Character dynamics at work.

jokes.text: Man, I remember my first time. It was everything a young man could dream of. And it only cost me 35 bucks.
jokes.setup: Jazz sets up romantic first time memory
jokes.punchline: Reveals he paid for it, subverting romantic expectation
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection structure. Jazz character fits, but the prostitute joke is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: But, of course, things were cheaper then.
jokes.setup: Jazz admits to paying $35 for first sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Treats inflation of prostitution prices as casual economic observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan follow-up that treats prostitution like any other commodity affected by inflation. Perfectly timed escalation.

jokes.text: Maybe he ain't got nothing to talk about. Lockjaw.
jokes.setup: Carlton stays quiet during sex talk
jokes.punchline: Jazz suggests Carlton has 'lockjaw' from oral sex inexperience
jokes.explanation: Crude sexual pun targeting Carlton's virginity. Functional but relies on cheap sexual humor.

jokes.text: I think he's more used to playing solitaire.
jokes.setup: Carlton says they're there to play poker
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton 'plays solitaire' (masturbates) instead
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre using card game terminology. Will's teasing of Carlton is character-appropriate and well-executed.

jokes.text: The only problem this stallion has is keeping the names with the faces...if you know what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to sound sexually experienced
jokes.punchline: Awkward bravado that rings completely false given his character
jokes.explanation: Carlton's attempt at player talk is hilariously unconvincing. Perfect character comedy showing his desperation to fit in.

jokes.text: Three kings and a virgin.
jokes.setup: Will shows his poker hand after Carlton's virginity speech
jokes.punchline: Describes cards as 'three kings and a virgin' pointing at Carlton
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that connects poker terminology with the conversation topic. Perfect timing and economy. One of Will's best zingers.

jokes.text: You'll be kissing more butt than Arsenio.
jokes.setup: Carlton needs to impress Princeton alum dean
jokes.punchline: Will compares it to Arsenio Hall's famous celebrity brown-nosing
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Arsenio's reputation. Functional but relies on pop culture reference rather than wit.

jokes.text: Carlton, please, forget about Miss Right. Go for Miss Right Now.
jokes.setup: Carlton talks about waiting for Miss Right
jokes.punchline: Will suggests 'Miss Right Now' instead - wordplay on timing vs standards
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay that encapsulates Will's philosophy vs Carlton's romanticism. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: She'll love everything I love. Money, Ivy League schools and America.
jokes.setup: Carlton describes his dream girl's personality
jokes.punchline: List escalates from shallow to absurdly patriotic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character comedy. The escalation from money to America is hilariously specific to his privileged Republican worldview.

jokes.text: Hey, lady, we were talking.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing dream girl when real woman approaches
jokes.punchline: Will rudely dismisses her, not realizing she matches Carlton's description
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dramatic irony - audience knows she's perfect for Carlton while Will dismisses her.

jokes.text: Come on, man, baby is all that. Go on, step to her and ask her out.
jokes.setup: Will just rudely dismissed the woman
jokes.punchline: Immediately does 180 and encourages Carlton to ask her out
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's quick assessment and change of strategy.

jokes.text: Not really. But my favorite singer is performing the national anthem. - Who's that? - Tom Jones.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if she's a Princeton fan
jokes.punchline: She's only interested because Tom Jones is singing anthem
jokes.explanation: Quirky character detail that Tom Jones is her favorite singer. Sets up their odd compatibility.

jokes.text: That girl's a damn thief. She stole that magazine. Hey!
jokes.setup: Will compliments Carlton's 'good catch'
jokes.punchline: Clerk reveals the girl literally stole something
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts Will's compliment about Carlton's romantic 'catch' with literal theft.

jokes.text: Bob, judges don't cry.
jokes.setup: Phil is on phone with emotional judge Bob
jokes.punchline: Phil's matter-of-fact statement about judicial dignity
jokes.explanation: Phil's authoritative yet caring tone perfect for character. Funny contrast between professional dignity and emotional crisis.

jokes.text: He's dead.
jokes.setup: Phil hanging up after comforting judge Bob
jokes.punchline: Flat declaration that he's dead (career-wise)
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient delivery. Great economy and dark humor about political destruction.

jokes.text: Hey, look. I found that Chevette. The body was already in the trunk too.
jokes.setup: Phil is worried about ethics investigation
jokes.punchline: Will casually mentions finding a car with a body in trunk
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Will's casual tone. Perfect timing given Phil's paranoia about scandals.

jokes.text: Stop persecuting me! I've done nothing, I tell you. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Will asks Geoffrey what's wrong
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dramatic overreaction to innocent question
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's paranoid guilt manifesting as dramatic overreaction. Sets up his upcoming confession nicely.

jokes.text: You just can't get good help these days.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey has dramatic outburst
jokes.punchline: Will dryly comments on servant quality like a rich person
jokes.explanation: Will ironically adopting the rich family's perspective on servant problems. Perfect character moment showing his adaptation.

jokes.text: You could come live at our house. My little sister is almost 15. It's time she moved out on her own.
jokes.setup: Joann mentions needing new apartment
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests kicking out his teenage sister
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Carlton's entitled worldview and desperation. The casualness of kicking out Ashley is hilariously self-centered.

jokes.text: Will you marry me?
jokes.setup: Carlton is having nice first date
jokes.punchline: Immediately proposes marriage
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton over-the-top romantic gesture. Cringe comedy from his complete lack of dating experience.

jokes.text: Tomorrow morning.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks for goodbye kiss
jokes.punchline: Joann suggests waiting until tomorrow morning (implying sex)
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with perfect economy. Two words that completely change the situation's trajectory.

jokes.text: Fine. [calls Bel-Air Hotel room service instead of making breakfast]
jokes.setup: Will suggests Hilary make her own breakfast
jokes.punchline: Hilary calls hotel room service instead of using kitchen
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag showing Hilary's complete helplessness and privileged mindset. Character-specific and well-executed.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that's my mom's finger. Get it off my hand.
jokes.setup: Carlton comes home wearing ring after losing virginity
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's wearing his mother's ring, panics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's post-sex panic. The mother's ring detail adds family dynamic humor.

jokes.text: So who put the offer on the table? - She did. And the terms were so attractive, I jumped on h...it. I jumped on it.
jokes.setup: Will uses business metaphors for sex
jokes.punchline: Carlton almost says 'jumped on her' then corrects to 'it'
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre setup from Will, Carlton's near-slip is believable character moment. Business metaphor works well.

jokes.text: I protected my investment.
jokes.setup: Carlton continues business metaphor for sex
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for using protection during sex
jokes.explanation: Continues the business metaphor effectively. Carlton's formal language applied to sex is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I protected my investment.
jokes.setup: Business metaphor for sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: References using protection in business terms
jokes.explanation: Continues the business metaphor cleverly. Good economy referring to safe sex.

jokes.text: Carlton lost his virginity.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks what they were talking about
jokes.punchline: Will states it matter-of-factly to young Ashley
jokes.explanation: Will's casual delivery of intimate information to Carlton's sister. Character-appropriate boundary-crossing.

jokes.text: That's all right, just don't take another morning off.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey apologizes for bringing shame on the house
jokes.punchline: Phil's main concern is work attendance, not shame
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil response - practical concerns over dramatic declarations. Shows his down-to-earth nature despite wealth.

jokes.text: You did a porno movie?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey says he's too ashamed to talk, better to show them
jokes.punchline: Will immediately assumes the worst possible scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that lets audience assume the worst before revealing the real (innocent) story. Will's assumption is hilariously specific.

jokes.text: ...was descended from a long line of running Butlers.
jokes.setup: Documentary about Geoffrey Butler, Olympic runner
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'running' (athletic) and family name Butler
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay connecting Geoffrey's profession and athletic history. The mock-documentary tone elevates it.

jokes.text: [Geoffrey takes taxi in the middle of marathon race]
jokes.setup: Geoffrey is losing Olympic marathon
jokes.punchline: Visual of him getting in taxi to cheat and win
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that's both absurd and perfectly logical for desperate situation. The taxi visual is comedy gold.

jokes.text: To his countrymen, he is the shame of a nation.
jokes.setup: Documentary about Geoffrey's Olympic cheating
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement of the consequences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making Geoffrey's minor cheating into national disgrace. Sets up his exile.

jokes.text: Hey, G, why don't you run into the kitchen...and get me something. Or better yet, take a cab.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's taxi-cheating scandal just revealed
jokes.punchline: Will immediately makes callback joke about taking cab instead of running
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing and delivery. Will immediately connects the scandal to practical jokes. Excellent character moment.

jokes.text: You're a servant. Get me some cake.
jokes.setup: Phil reassures Geoffrey the committee won't care about his scandal
jokes.punchline: Casually orders cake while making the point about class difference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil moment - reassuring but also casually reinforcing hierarchy. The timing of the cake order is perfect.

jokes.text: Green card? What green card?
jokes.setup: Will asking when Geoffrey got his green card
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals he's an illegal immigrant
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - turns out Geoffrey's real secret is immigration status, not Olympic shame.

jokes.text: You are what you eat, buddy.
jokes.setup: Someone offers Will a weenie at reception
jokes.punchline: Will uses 'you are what you eat' to call them a weenie/loser
jokes.explanation: Clever use of common saying as insult. Will's quick wit on display, though the setup is somewhat contrived.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah. She gave you a key chain.
jokes.setup: Will starts telling his first time story again
jokes.punchline: Carlton references Will's earlier sarcastic keychain comment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Carlton learned Will's sarcastic style. Character growth moment wrapped in humor.

jokes.text: You want me to sleep with him?
jokes.setup: Will says 'when you want something, go for it' about approaching dean
jokes.punchline: Carlton misinterprets as sexual suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Carlton's new sexual confidence affecting his interpretation. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Really? Well, quelle surprise.
jokes.setup: Dean mentions Princeton is his alma mater (obviously)
jokes.punchline: Carlton uses French to sarcastically express fake surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton pretentiousness - using French for sarcasm. Shows his newfound confidence affecting his personality.

jokes.text: Hey, it is great to see you two together again.
jokes.setup: Carlton meets Dean's wife Joann (the woman he slept with)
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward attempt to defuse the situation
jokes.explanation: Will's panic-driven attempt at damage control. The awkwardness is the comedy - perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Well, who is it? - It's your uncle. - My only uncle died three years ago. - Then you should take the call because it's probably really long-distance.
jokes.setup: Will lies about phone call to separate Dean and Carlton
jokes.punchline: When caught in lie, doubles down with absurd long-distance dead uncle joke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Will's panic leads to increasingly absurd lies. The dead uncle long-distance call is comedy gold.

jokes.text: It's your uncle. My only uncle died three years ago. Then you should take the call because it's probably really long-distance.
jokes.setup: Will claiming there's phone call from dean's uncle
jokes.punchline: When told uncle is dead, suggests call is from beyond grave
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor escalation. Will commits to the lie even when exposed. Great timing and delivery.

jokes.text: You have to go through this every time you come up here? No, I take the ladder.
jokes.setup: Will asking about climbing to treehouse
jokes.punchline: Carlton's matter-of-fact response about ladder
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response. Carlton being practical even in his emotional crisis.

jokes.text: Of all the students in all the universities...in all the world, she had to sleep with me.
jokes.setup: Carlton is devastated about Joann situation
jokes.punchline: Parodies Casablanca with his specific romantic tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Casablanca parody that fits Carlton's dramatic romantic nature. Classic reference elevated by specific situation.

jokes.text: That would be what, rocks?
jokes.setup: Carlton says he feels 'lower than dirt'
jokes.punchline: Will literally interprets what's lower than dirt geologically
jokes.explanation: Will's literal interpretation of Carlton's metaphor. Perfect timing to deflate dramatic moment with logic.

jokes.text: How about we go down and we discuss this on solid ground, huh? I'll buy you some rocky road, baby.
jokes.setup: Carlton is upset in tree house, Will wants him to come down
jokes.punchline: Offers 'rocky road' ice cream, playing on the rock/ground metaphors
jokes.explanation: Clever continuation of rock/ground metaphors with ice cream. Will's attempt to cheer Carlton up through wordplay.

jokes.text: Down the hall, third door on your right.
jokes.setup: Will asks what Carlton will do when he needs bathroom in tree house
jokes.punchline: Carlton gives house directions as if tree house is connected
jokes.explanation: Absurd practical detail that makes tree house seem like extension of house. Perfect deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: I'm so stupid. I'm a stupid, dirty rock.
jokes.setup: Earlier established Carlton feels like 'really dirty rocks'
jokes.punchline: Carlton now identifies as stupid dirty rock
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the rock metaphor while showing Carlton's continued distress.

jokes.text: Well, there's a first time for everything. Of course, you know that.
jokes.setup: Will admits he was wrong for first time
jokes.punchline: Carlton references Will losing his virginity as another 'first time'
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that turns Will's admission into sexual reference. Shows Carlton's growing wit and confidence.

jokes.text: That's next year's Emmy right there.
jokes.setup: Will breaks character after flubbing line
jokes.punchline: Self-referential joke about the blooper being Emmy-worthy
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor breaking fourth wall. Acknowledges the blooper as part of the show's charm.
